skill.name: airunway-aks-setup
skill.description: Set up AI Runway on AKS — from bare cluster to running model. Covers cluster verification, controller install, GPU assessment, provider setup, and first deployment. WHEN: "setup AI Runway", "onboard AKS cluster", "install AI Runway", "airunway setup", "deploy model to AKS", "GPU inference on AKS", "KAITO setup on AKS", "run LLM on AKS", "vLLM on AKS", "set up model serving on AKS", "AI Runway controller".
skill.summary: Set up AI Runway on AKS from bare cluster to running model deployment with GPU assessment and inference provider setup
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Microsoft-published documentation skill for AI Runway AKS setup. Static scanner flagged documentation files containing bash/PowerShell code examples as potential security issues. After evaluation, all findings are false positives: the skill provides markdown documentation with command examples for human execution, not executable code. No actual command injection, path traversal vulnerabilities, or malicious patterns exist. The skill is safe for publication with low risk level.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Examples Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged markdown files with bash/PowerShell code blocks as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are documentation files providing command examples for users to execute manually. No actual shell execution occurs. Pattern matchers cannot distinguish between executable code and human-readable documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Code blocks in markdown documentation are clearly delineated as examples for users to run manually. The skill metadata explicitly states 'All cluster operations are performed directly via kubectl and make' - indicating this is a guide skill, not executable code.

content.user_title: Set up AI Runway on AKS from bare cluster to running model
content.value_statement: Deploying LLMs on Azure Kubernetes Service requires orchestrating multiple components including GPU nodes, inference providers, and model artifacts. This skill guides you through the complete setup process from cluster verification to your first working model deployment.
actual_capabilities: Verify AKS cluster connectivity, node inventory, and GPU availability
Install AI Runway controller and Custom Resource Definitions
Assess GPU hardware compatibility and identify dtype constraints
Recommend and configure inference providers (KAITO, Dynamo, KubeRay)
Deploy AI models to AKS with automatic endpoint creation
Execute smoke tests to verify model readiness and accessibility
limitations: Requires an existing AKS cluster — cluster provisioning is handled by the azure-kubernetes skill
GPU node pools incur significant Azure compute costs (A100-80GB can cost $3-5+ per hour)
Does not provide MCP tools — all operations use kubectl and make via user execution

use_cases.title: First-time AI Runway deployment
use_cases.description: New to AI Runway on AKS. Get a complete walkthrough from cluster verification through your first working model deployment with GPU acceleration.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers setting up AI inference on Azure

use_cases.title: GPU capability assessment
use_cases.description: Discover available GPU hardware, check dtype support (bfloat16, float16), and receive model recommendations based on your cluster VRAM capacity.
use_cases.target_user: ML engineers optimizing model selection for available hardware

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot failed deployments
use_cases.description: Resume from a specific step after partial setup, or follow rollback procedures to undo a failed deployment and start fresh.
use_cases.target_user: Engineers recovering from interrupted or failed setups

prompt_templates.title: Basic AI Runway Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up AI Runway on my AKS cluster. I have an existing cluster with GPU nodes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Standard first-time setup with GPU nodes

prompt_templates.title: Resume from Specific Step
prompt_templates.prompt: Skip to step 4 and set up the KAITO inference provider on my AKS cluster.

prompt_templates.title: GPU Assessment Only
prompt_templates.prompt: Check what GPUs are available in my AKS cluster and tell me which models I can run.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy Specific Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy the Llama-3.1-8B model to my AKS cluster using AI Runway. I have an A100-80GB node.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deploy a specific gated model with known GPU capacity

output_examples.input: Set up AI Runway on my AKS cluster
output_examples.output:
Step 1 - Cluster Verification:
✓ kubectl found
✓ make found
✓ curl found
✓ Cluster context: my-cluster
✓ Nodes: 3 total, 1 GPU node detected
  - GPU Node: Standard_A100_80GB
  - VRAM: 80 GB
  - bfloat16: Supported

Ready to proceed to Step 2?

output_examples.input: Deploy a model with 8B parameters on my cluster
output_examples.output:
Recommendation: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
Provider: KAITO (vLLM)
Reason: Your A100-80GB has capacity for 8B models with tensor parallel options.


This is a gated model requiring a HuggingFace access token. Shall I proceed with token collection?

best_practices: Always confirm GPU node availability and VRAM capacity before selecting a model size
Start with non-gated models like Phi-3 or Gemma to validate the setup before using gated models
Use the skip-to-step parameter to resume from specific steps after interruptions
anti_patterns: Do not run this skill without first confirming you understand GPU compute costs on Azure
Do not skip cluster verification — understanding your GPU hardware is required for model selection
Do not attempt gated models (Llama, etc.) until you have validated the setup with a non-gated model

faq.question: What is AI Runway?
faq.answer: AI Runway is a Kubernetes-native framework for deploying and managing LLM inference on Azure Kubernetes Service. It provides Custom Resource Definitions for model deployments and integrates with inference providers like KAITO, Dynamo, and KubeRay.

faq.question: Do I need an existing AKS cluster?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill assumes you have an existing AKS cluster. If you need to create one, use the azure-kubernetes skill first to provision a cluster with GPU nodes, then return to this skill.

faq.question: What GPUs are supported?
faq.answer: AI Runway supports NVIDIA GPUs including T4, V100, A10, A10G, L4, L40S, A100, H100, and H200. Each has different dtype support — older GPUs like T4 and V100 do not support bfloat16.

faq.question: Why did my deployment fail with bfloat16 errors?
faq.answer: T4 and V100 GPUs do not support bfloat16 precision. Add --dtype float16 to serving arguments, or switch to xformers attention backend. Check the gpu-profiles.md reference for your specific GPU constraints.

faq.question: How long does model deployment take?
faq.answer: Small models (1B-8B) typically deploy in 5-10 minutes. Large models (70B+) may take 20-40+ minutes because model weights must be downloaded from HuggingFace first. Check pod logs for download progress.

faq.question: How do I rollback a failed deployment?
faq.answer: Use kubectl delete for model deployments and secrets. For providers and controllers, use make commands from the AI Runway repo root. See troubleshooting.md for the complete rollback sequence.
